answer: The capital of Canada is Ottawa, located in the province of Ontario. It was chosen as the capital in 1867. Ottawa is the political center of the country.

results.title: Canada's Capitals
results.snippet: # Canada’s Capitals. Canada’s capitals are alive with stories that emphasize our history, our heritage and our cultures. Fredericton is the capital of the province of New Brunswick and is home to over 56,224 residents. Iqaluit is Nunavut’s capital and Canada’s coolest arctic city! Ottawa, Canada’s National Capital is situated at the confluence of the majestic Rideau, Gatineau and des Outaouais, and is home to over 900,000 residents — making it the fourth-largest city in Canada. Quebec City is the capital of the Province of Quebec and is home to over 516,000 people. Established as a City in 1834, Toronto became the capital of Ontario when the Province was created at Confederation in 1867. Established as the Yukon's capital city in 1953, the City of Whitehorse is home to some of the most spectacular scenery in Canada. Yellowknife is a culturally rich capital thriving with diversity, and home to about 20,000 people.

results.title: Capital of Canada: Why Ottawa?
results.snippet: # Capital of Canada: Why Ottawa? # Capital of Canada: Why Ottawa? Capital of Canada: Why Ottawa? Creating the Province of Canada prompted the need of a single capital city. ## Creating the Province of Canada prompted the need of a single capital city, however, the pre-Confederation colonies of Upper and Lower Canada could not agree on a permanent site to establish this. When the Province of Canada was established, Kingston was initially selected as the capital city. In 1867, the Dominion of Canada was established, and Ottawa continued to serve as the capital. ### Why Ottawa Remains the Capital City. Over the years, Ottawa has grown into a vibrant and dynamic capital city, embodying Canada's values of diversity, inclusivity, and cooperation. Have students create profiles for each potential capital city, considering factors like geographical location, cultural composition, and economic significance. You can also visit one of the Canada’s former capitals of Kingston, Toronto, Québec City or Montréal on a Brightspark tour!
